channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Oil prices fall but remain near one-month highs amid Gulf tensions
item.description: Oil prices edged lower on 16 July, Thursday, as market participants assessed growing tensions between the US and Iran, and the potential impact on oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: USA Crude Oil Stocks Drop Almost 2MM Barrels WoW
item.description: Crude oil stocks, not including the SPR, stood at 409.7 million barrels on July 10, the EIA's latest weekly petroleum status report showed.

item.title: Delfin issues LNTP to Siemens Energy for FLNG2 vessel
item.description: Delfin Midstream has given a Limited Notice to Proceed (LNTP) to Siemens Energy for the purchase of several major components for its second floating liquefied natural gas (LNG) production vessel, known as FLNG2.

item.title: India Bars Seafarers from Hormuz Voyages
item.description: India has directed ship owners, ship managers and recruitment agencies to stop deploying the country's seafarers on vessels going through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: USA-Iran Skirmishes Worsen as Hormuz Traffic Dwindles
item.description: The U.S. struck Iran for a fifth straight day overnight and hit a sanctioned oil tanker near the country's main export terminal.

item.title: Halliburton wins unconventional gas project contract from Aramco
item.description: Halliburton has secured a multi-year contract from Aramco to provide integrated stimulation and completion services for unconventional gas development in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Pakistan Pays Highest Spot LNG Price in Four Years as Qatar Supply Falters
item.description: State-controlled Pakistan LNG Ltd has bought the most expensive cargo of liquefied natural gas on the spot market in four years as the recent re-escalation of the Hormuz crisis cut off cargoes from its term supplier…

item.title: TotalEnergies Sees Stronger Q2 Profit as Refining and Oil Trading Surge
item.description: TotalEnergies expects higher cash flows from its oil production and strong refining margins and oil trading results to boost second-quarter profits compared to the prior quarter, as oil prices rose and fuel markets…

item.title: India Urges Shipowners to Avoid Deploying Nationals on Hormuz Voyages
item.description: India has advised ship owners and managers to avoid hiring Indian nationals on vessels they plan to send through the Strait of Hormuz, as attacks on commercial vessels have intensified in the area since the U.S.-Iran…

item.title: India Hikes Diesel and Jet Fuel Export Tax
item.description: India has hiked the export duties it will be charging on exports of diesel and jet fuel for the two weeks starting July 16, as the return of the Hormuz crisis is set to further tighten fuel markets. The export taxes on…

item.title: IEA Warns World Has Just Weeks to Avoid Hormuz Economic Shock
item.description: The world has just a few weeks before taking an economic toll from the re-escalation in the Middle East and the new halt to tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, according to Fatih Birol, the Executive Director…

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran-Linked Tanker Near Kharg Island
item.description: U.S. forces have struck and disabled an Iran-linked sanctioned oil tanker near Iran’s key export terminal, Kharg Island, deep in the Persian Gulf, the U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) said early on Thursday, as the U.S…

item.title: Oil Settles Higher
item.description: Oil prices rose as the US intensified strikes on Iran, heightening concerns over Middle East crude supplies despite weaker US fuel demand data.

item.title: Global Investments In Nuclear Fusion Surge 69% To $4.5B
item.description: Global private investments in nuclear fusion hit a record $4.48 billion in 2025, in large part driven by the booming energy demands of AI data centers and rising global energy security concerns. According to the Fusion…

item.title: As Ukraine Cripples Russian Refining, Global Diesel Markets Pay the Price
item.description: Since March, Ukraine has systematically dismantled Russia’s refining system, driving crude processing to its lowest level in 21 years and forcing Moscow to ban exports of gasoline, jet fuel and diesel. Russian…

item.title: AI Boom Sends U.S. Electricity Demand to New High
item.description: U.S. electricity consumption set a new record high last year and is on track for new all-time highs this year and next, as the AI boom has ended America’s decade of stagnant power use. Rising electrification rates are…

item.title: Five Oil and Gas Stocks Ready for a Hormuz Spike and a Hawkish Fed
item.description: Two shocks are hitting energy investors at once, and they point in opposite directions. The first is obvious. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on July 11, the U.S. answered with…

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran Again as Brent Slips Despite Escalating Conflict
item.description: The oil market is treating another US-Iran escalation as background noise. The U.S. launched another wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, expanding attacks on military targets along the country's southern coast…

item.title: China Just Connected the World's Biggest Hybrid Solar Plant to the Grid
item.description: The face of energy security is changing. Historically, fossil fuels have represented security thanks to their established supply chains and round-the-clock production potential, while renewable energies represented risk…

item.title: USA EIA Raises Henry Hub Price Forecast for 2026, 2027
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration raised its Henry Hub natural gas spot price projection for 2026 and 2027 in its latest short term energy outlook.
